questions.question: What was the name of the German encryption machine that Alan Turing and his team at Bletchley Park worked to crack during World War II?
questions.explanation: The Enigma machine was used by the German military for secure communications; Allied codebreakers decrypted many of its messages, significantly aiding the war effort.

presentation.questionStyled: Name the German encryption device that Turing’s team famously outsmarted during WWII.
presentation.explanationStyled: It was Enigma. Turns out, even “unbreakable” machines have limits when humans start thinking.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Let’s see if history left any impression at all.
wrongAnswerQuips: That’s not even the right war.
Impressive confidence. Shame about the accuracy.

questions.question: Which Norse explorer is widely believed to have reached North America centuries before Columbus?
questions.explanation: Leif Erikson reached North America around the year 1000, likely landing in present-day Newfoundland.

presentation.questionStyled: Which Viking beat Columbus to North America by a few centuries—no fanfare, just vibes?
presentation.explanationStyled: Leif Erikson. Quietly got there first. No parade, no PR team, just history doing its thing.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Let’s revisit the part of history people conveniently forget.
wrongAnswerQuips: That one stayed in the sagas, not the Americas.
Wrong Viking. There are a few, try again.
You picked the wrong longboat captain.

questions.question: In CRISPR-Cas9, what does the 'P' stand for?
questions.explanation: CRISPR stands for Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats.

presentation.questionStyled: CRISPR isn’t just a buzzword. What does the 'P' actually stand for?
presentation.explanationStyled: Palindromic. Yes, like words that read the same backward. Science can be poetic when it wants to be.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Let’s see if you know the science behind the headlines.
wrongAnswerQuips: That sounds scientific, but it’s wrong.
You just picked the most intimidating word, didn’t you?
Nope. Try again, but maybe think this time.

questions.question: Which neurotransmitter plays a key role in reward and motor control?
questions.explanation: Dopamine is central to reward pathways and motor function; its depletion is linked to Parkinson’s disease.

presentation.questionStyled: Which brain chemical keeps you motivated—and able to move like a functional human?
presentation.explanationStyled: Dopamine. No dopamine, no drive… and possibly no smooth movement either.
wrongAnswerQuips: Not the one keeping you going.

questions.question: Which particle discovered in 2012 at CERN is often called the 'God Particle'?
questions.explanation: The Higgs Boson explains how particles acquire mass and completed the Standard Model.

presentation.questionStyled: Which particle discovery in 2012 made physicists collectively lose their minds—in a good way?
presentation.explanationStyled: Higgs Boson. The missing piece finally showed up. Took long enough.
presentation.hostLeadIn: Time to see if physics stuck at all.
wrongAnswerQuips: Wrong particle. Try a bigger headline.
Nope. That one wasn’t worth a global celebration.
